name: Punta Umbría and Enebrales Coastal Loop
name_original: Carril cicloturista Salinas del Astur – Ribera de Punta Umbría – circular desde Nuevo Portil
description: Cycle a moderate 19.0-mile route from Nuevo Portil, exploring the Odiel Marshes, Umbría Tower, and coastal views in Huelva.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cartaya traverse a diverse landscape on the Huelva coast of Andalusia, Spain. The region features extensive pine forests, significant wetlands like the Marismas del Río Piedras y Flecha del Rompido Natural Area, and 14 km of fine sand beaches. This varied terrain offers numerous opportunities for cycling, with routes often following the coast or winding through natural parks. The area's flat to gently rolling topography makes it suitable for various cycling abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Cartaya The most popular touring cycling route is Punta Umbría and Enebrales Coastal Loop, a moderate 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route explores the coastal environment and the Enebrales area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is El Portil Beach – La Bota Beach loop from Urbanización Hoyo 19, an easy 14.0 miles (22.5 km) path. This route offers views of the beaches and coastal scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the View of the Piedras River – View of the Flecha del Rompido loop from El Portil, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail leading through the Piedras River estuary and past the Flecha del Rompido, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Cartaya is defined by coastal paths, extensive pine forests, and river estuaries. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to more moderate routes through natural areas. The routes in Cartaya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cartaya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cartaya?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes to explore in and around Cartaya, offering a wide variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cartaya?
A: Cartaya enjoys a mild climate with abundant sunshine throughout the year, featuring approximately 3,000 hours of sun and an average annual temperature of 25ºC. This makes it an ideal destination for touring cycling year-round, though spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Cartaya?
A: Yes, Cartaya offers a good selection of easy routes. There are 12 routes specifically rated as easy, perfect for beginners or family outings. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14585020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Portil Beach – La Bota Beach loop from Urbanización Hoyo 19&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 14.0-mile path offering pleasant coastal views.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I experience on touring cycling routes in Cartaya?
A: Touring cycling routes in Cartaya showcase a diverse landscape. You'll find extensive pine forests, significant wetlands like the Marismas del Río Piedras y Flecha del Rompido Natural Area, and 14 km of fine sand beaches. Many routes follow the coast or wind through natural parks, offering a mix of coastal paths, forest trails, and river estuaries.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Cartaya?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Cartaya are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984888425/punta-umbria-and-enebrales-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Umbría and Enebrales Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 19.0-mile trail that explores the coastal environment.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can see along the routes?
A: Cartaya's routes offer numerous scenic spots. You can enjoy panoramic views of the Marismas del Río Piedras y Flecha del Rompido Natural Area, especially from viewpoints like San Miguel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33889773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Piedras River – View of the Flecha del Rompido loop from El Portil&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the estuary and past the unique sandy formation. You might also encounter the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/982208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Portil Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, a haven for birds.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by bike?
A: Yes, you can cycle to several historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31585582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Zúñiga – La Antilla Beach loop from Nuevo Portil&lt;/a&gt; allows you to visit the 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Zúñiga&lt;/a&gt;. The picturesque fishing village of El Rompido, with its lighthouse, is also a charming destination.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cartaya?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cartaya are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from coastal paths to pine forests, and the well-maintained trails that cater to various abilities.

Q: Can I find routes that specifically explore the El Rompido area?
A: Absolutely. El Rompido is a popular starting point for cycling. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17221925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rompido Lighthouse – Castillo de los Zúñiga loop from El Rompido&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12.4-mile route that allows you to explore the charming fishing village and its surroundings.

Q: Are there options for more challenging or advanced touring cycling in Cartaya?
A: While many routes are easy to moderate, Cartaya does offer options for cyclists with good fitness levels. There are 10 routes rated as difficult, often featuring longer distances or more varied terrain, including some unpaved segments through areas like the Cartaya Pine Forest. These can involve elevation gains of over 1500 feet.

Q: Are there any less crowded bike paths in Cartaya?
A: To find less crowded paths, consider exploring routes that venture deeper into the Cartaya Pine Forest or the Marismas del Río Piedras y Flecha del Rompido Natural Area during off-peak times. These natural areas offer extensive networks of trails where you can often find tranquility away from more popular coastal stretches.
tips:
This natural area covers approximately 7,185 hectares and is known for its rich biodiversity and its importance as a breeding area and refuge for numerous bird species.

Notable species include the Eurasian spoonbill, the flamingo, various ducks, grey and purple herons, and birds of prey such as the osprey. The area also has an interpretation center, birdwatching points, trails, and rest areas.
12th century tower declared an Asset of Cultural Interest and protected as historical heritage. Built to face the Barbary pirates.

This beach bar is a good reference for eating and taking a bath. The owner, Paco, makes it easy for you to leave the bike in a safe place and to change your clothes in the same place. It is eaten very well and at a moderate price. To finish, pay yourself a tribute by trying one of their delicious mojitos.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route from Nuevo Portil?
faqs.answer: The route is circular from Nuevo Portil, so you can typically find parking within or near the town to begin your ride. Look for designated parking areas in Nuevo Portil, especially those catering to visitors or near the start of local cycling paths.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cycling route Salinas del Astur – Ribera de Punta Umbría?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly paved surfaces, making it suitable for various bicycle types like road bikes, gravel bikes, and e-bikes. While largely paved, some segments may include unpaved sections. The region offers a mix of terrains, from flat coastal paths to areas with gentle slopes.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate and requires a good fitness level. While generally flat, with an elevation gain of 80 meters over 30.6 km, the moderate rating implies some segments might be more challenging due to slight inclines, wind conditions, or potential unpaved sections. It might be challenging for absolute beginners or very young children, but suitable for families with good cycling experience.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse scenery, including views of marshes and pine forests. Notable natural areas include the Odiel Marshes, a Biosphere Reserve known for birdwatching, and areas near the Laguna del Portil. You can also expect to see the Umbría Tower and enjoy the golden sand beaches of the Costa de la Luz near Punta Umbría.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Huelva province generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summer can be warm, especially midday, so consider early morning or late afternoon rides during those months. Winter is typically mild, making it a good option for escaping colder climates.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Cycling route Salinas del Astur – Ribera de Punta Umbría?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact route regarding dogs are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on many cycling paths in the region, especially if kept on a leash. When passing through natural parks or protected areas, always ensure your dog is under control and follow any posted signage regarding pet regulations to protect local wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle through the natural areas?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near protected natural areas like the Odiel Marshes and the Paraje Natural Los Enebrales de Punta Umbría. Generally, for cycling on established paths within these areas, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for individual recreational use. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked trails, and avoid disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails in the Huelva area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of a broader network of cycling routes in Huelva. Specifically, it includes sections of official trails such as La Bota - El Portil, Camino Verde Huelva - Punta Umbría VM-497, and Sendero de la Laguna de El Portil, offering connections to other paths and natural sites in the region.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route or near Nuevo Portil?
faqs.answer: Yes, being a popular coastal area, there are options for refreshments. Near Punta Umbría, you might find places like Chiringuito Oliver. Nuevo Portil and Punta Umbría offer various cafes, restaurants, and chiringuitos (beach bars) where you can stop for a meal or a drink.

faqs.question: What kind of bicycle is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mostly paved surfaces with some potential unpaved segments, a touring bicycle, gravel bike, or e-bike would be ideal. Road bikes are also suitable for the paved sections, but riders might find the unpaved parts more challenging. Mountain bikes would handle all terrains comfortably, though might be slower on the paved sections.
